name: Prime Alliance Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured bank offering personal and business checking, savings, money market accounts, and CDs with competitive rates and personalized local service.
description_long:
Prime Alliance Bank operates as a traditional FDIC-insured bank providing deposit and lending products to both personal and business customers. The institution emphasizes personalized service through local, experienced employees rather than purely digital banking channels. Based on their website messaging, they position themselves as relationship-focused, particularly for business lending.

Their personal banking products include Interest Checking (0.20% APY), Savings Accounts (up to 3.75% APY), Money Market Accounts (up to 3.75% APY), CDs with fixed rates, and IRAs. For business customers, they offer Keep It Simple Business Checking, Business Interest Checking, Business Savings, Business Money Market, and Commercial/SBA financing solutions. They provide nationwide ATM access, mobile banking, debit cards, and direct deposit functionality.

Prime Alliance differentiates itself through emphasis on personalized attention and creative problem-solving, particularly highlighted in their testimonial from a business customer noting "more than ten years" of partnership. They advertise competitive CD and savings rates and claim to work with customers on tailored financial solutions. Their routing number is 124303081, and their main phone line is (801) 296-2200.

As a traditional bank, Prime Alliance offers fundamental deposit products and business lending, but their website lacks detailed rate tables, fee schedules, loan terms, or minimum deposit requirements. The available information is limited and marketing-focused rather than comprehensive. No specific information about APRs on commercial loans, early withdrawal penalties, account minimums, or full fee structures is provided on the accessible pages. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Mobile banking and online account access
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. Government
Competitive savings rates advertised up to 3.75% APY
Nationwide ATM access with no surcharges mentioned
Dedicated business lending with SBA financing options
Mobile banking and online account opening available
Personalized local service with experienced staff
cons: Limited transparency on website—no detailed fee schedule or account minimums disclosed
Interest checking rates very low (0.20% APY) compared to online banks
No specific information on commercial loan terms, rates, or APRs
No online rate comparison or product selection tools available
best_for: Business owners seeking relationship-based lending and commercial financing
Customers prioritizing personalized local service over online-only banking
Small businesses needing SBA loan guidance and tailored financing solutions
Savers seeking FDIC-insured accounts with competitive rates
diagnosis: Prime Alliance Bank is best suited for small business owners and savers who value personalized, relationship-based banking and prefer working directly with experienced local loan officers, particularly for commercial or SBA financing. The main caveat is the bank's limited digital transparency—critical information like fees, minimums, and exact loan terms are not readily available online, requiring customers to call or visit in person to compare products.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
